title: cjkcodecs: MultibyteIncrementalDecoder.getstate() races with reset() on the decoder pending state
description: multibytecodec.c's incremental decoder keeps its incomplete-input state in plain fields (pending[8], pendingsize, state) with no lock/critical section; a decoder shared across threads calling getstate() (reads self->pendingsize) and reset() (writes self->pendingsize=0) concurrently is a data race on self->pendingsize.
sites: Modules/cjkcodecs/multibytecodec.c _multibytecodec_MultibyteIncrementalDecoder_getstate_impl:1261 (read self->pendingsize / self->pending, arg to PyBytes_FromStringAndSize)
race_kind: unsynchronized mutable object state (no critical section / GIL-era stateful C object)
shared_state: MultibyteIncrementalDecoderObject: unsigned char pending[8], Py_ssize_t pendingsize, MultibyteCodec_State state (all in _MultibyteStatefulDecoder_HEAD, multibytecodec.h); reported race is on pendingsize (8-byte Py_ssize_t)
severity: low-medium (reported getstate/reset pairing is value-benign & crash-free; the same unguarded fields are memcpy'd by decode()/setstate() into a fixed 8-byte buffer, so the wider family can tear pending vs pendingsize)
repro_reliability: deterministic (exit 66, races within ~1-2s); signature matches seed exactly (getstate_impl:1261 read vs reset_impl:1346 write)
suggested_fix: add per-object critical sections to the incremental encoder/decoder + stream reader/writer methods (Argument Clinic @critical_section on decode/getstate/setstate/reset, or Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(self)); a lone atomic on pendingsize is insufficient because pending+pendingsize+state form one logical state that must be accessed as a unit
notes: multibytecodec.c has ZERO Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION and no @critical_section clinic annotations; the whole incremental/stream CJK codec family shares the _MultibyteStatefulDecoder/Encoder_HEAD layout and the same unguarded-access pattern. Classic 'stateful stdlib C object never made FT-safe' race, not a specific already-filed bug. Fix should cover the whole family.

related_issues.note: multibytecodec errors-setter race; PR #153000 fixes ONLY codecctx_errors_set, NOT getstate/reset — this pending-state race is distinct and unfixed
Modules/cjkcodecs/multibytecodec.c is UNCHECKED on the gh-116738 audit list (whole file has zero critical sections/atomics on current main) — this race is exactly what that audit would surface.
